front: What is an uncaught exception in Java?
back: An uncaught exception is an exception that is thrown during the execution of a program but is not handled by any catch block. It propagates up the call stack until it is either caught or terminates the program.

front: What is the default behavior of the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) when an uncaught exception occurs in the main thread?
back: The JVM calls the `printStackTrace()` method of the uncaught exception, prints the stack trace to the standard error stream, and then terminates the program.

front: What is the role of the `Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler` interface?
back: It provides a mechanism to handle uncaught exceptions in a thread. You can set a custom handler for a thread to define what should happen when an uncaught exception causes the thread to terminate.

front: How can you set a default uncaught exception handler for all threads in your application?
back: By using the static method `Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler eh)` to set a handler that will be invoked for any thread that does not have its own specific handler.

front: What is a common use case for implementing a custom `UncaughtExceptionHandler`?
back: To log the exception details to a file or a logging service before the thread terminates, providing a more robust way to track errors than the default console output. It is also used for cleaning up resources or notifying users of the failure.
